---
title: "Getting Started"
description: "Set up your learning environment and understand how Python runs before memorizing syntax."
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Why this chapter matters

Set up your learning environment and understand how Python runs before memorizing syntax.

Lessons are now grouped by coherent learner topics. A page may contain multiple
short quizzes when the page has multiple sections, so students can check one
idea before moving to the next.

::: {.questions}
### Questions

- Which lessons belong in this chapter?
- What should I review before moving on?
- How do the lesson sections build one practical skill?
:::

## Lesson path

- [What Is Programming?](what-is-programming.qmd) — Understand programs, Python, interpreters, and the beginner learning loop.
- [Google Colab](google-colab.qmd) — Run Python in a browser and learn the notebook workflow without installing software.
- [Local Python Tools](local-python-tools.qmd) — Install Python and use VS Code, Jupyter, and the terminal as one local learning toolkit.

## Chapter checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this short checkpoint before starting the chapter.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "getting-started-overview",
  "title": "Getting Started overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "chapter-purpose",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the Getting Started chapter?",
      "options": [
        "Set up your learning environment and understand how Python runs before memorizing syntax.",
        "To memorize disconnected commands",
        "To skip practice",
        "To avoid debugging"
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Set up your learning environment and understand how Python runs before memorizing syntax."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-flow",
      "prompt": "Why can a lesson contain multiple quizzes?",
      "options": [
        "Each quiz checks one section before the next section builds on it.",
        "Quizzes replace examples.",
        "Multiple quizzes mean the topic is unrelated.",
        "Quizzes should only appear at the end of the course."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Section-level quizzes help learners review while the idea is still fresh."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
